---
pagetitle: "Start with a Design-Loop Card - Architecture 2.0"
description: "Draft a Level 0 Architecture 2.0 design-loop card in 30 minutes, then progress with evidence through Levels 1 to 3."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap start-page">
<header class="site-masthead">
  <span class="pill">Architecture 2.0</span>
  <span class="pill hot">Level 0 first</span>
</header>

<section class="site-hero start-hero">
  <div class="hub-kicker">Start with the core artifact</div>
  <h1>Draft one design-loop card</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">
    Name the decision, bound the design space, and make the claim you intend to
    test visible. Add audit, replay, and independent review only when the
    corresponding records exist.
  </p>
  <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Design-loop card starting points">
    <a class="site-action" href="/design-loop-card/template.yaml" download>Download Level 0 YAML <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&darr;</span></a>
    <a class="site-action secondary" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/level-0-context.yaml">Inspect Level 0 example</a>
    <a class="site-action tertiary" href="/book/appendices/appendix-b-design-loop-card/">Read Appendix B</a>
  </nav>
</section>

<section class="start-workflow" aria-labelledby="draft-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="draft-title">Draft Level 0 in 30 minutes</h2>
    <p>Level 0 records context and boundaries. It is a useful planning artifact, but it does not yet contain the records required for a Level 1 audit.</p>
  </div>

  <ol class="start-step-list">
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">01</span>
        <div><p>5 minutes</p><h3>Choose one decision</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Write one bounded architecture decision or experiment. Avoid goals such as &ldquo;improve the system&rdquo; that do not identify what could advance next.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">02</span>
        <div><p>10 minutes</p><h3>State intent and task</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Name the objective, constraints, non-goals, and bounded task. Put a paper's exact claim and non-claim under <code>intent.claim_boundary</code>. It is a view of intent, not a thirteenth field.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">03</span>
        <div><p>10 minutes</p><h3>Draw the design-space boundary</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>List legal choices, invalid choices, and work deferred to a later turn. A reviewer should be able to tell where this loop must stop.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">04</span>
        <div><p>5 minutes</p><h3>Validate Level 0 structure</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Run the validator from a repository clone and fix every reported path.</p>
      <pre class="start-command"><code>.venv-card/bin/python cli/arch2.py validate card my-card.yaml</code></pre>
    </li>
  </ol>
</section>

<aside class="site-note" aria-labelledby="validation-limit-title">
  <span class="site-note-label">Interpretation limit</span>
  <p id="validation-limit-title"><strong>Schema validation proves structure, not evidence quality.</strong> A passing card has the fields and level constraints required by the contract. It does not prove that the evidence is adequate, a referenced replay succeeds, or independent reviewers agree with the claim or commitment.</p>
</aside>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="progress-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="progress-title">Progress with existing evidence</h2>
    <p>The four examples follow one unchanged accelerator-array case. Each level preserves the earlier context and adds the records needed for a stronger conformance claim. Do not invent evidence to raise the level.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/level-0-context.yaml">
      <span class="site-pill">Draft</span>
      <strong>Level 0, context</strong>
      <span>Intent, task, and legal, invalid, and deferred design choices</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/level-1-auditable.yaml">
      <span class="site-pill">Add records</span>
      <strong>Level 1, auditable card</strong>
      <span>Representation, environment, actor map, feedback budget, evidence, and failed-run or rejected-alternative entries</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/level-2-replayable.yaml">
      <span class="site-pill">Bind replay</span>
      <strong>Level 2, replay bindings present</strong>
      <span>Stable IDs, workload and seed, tool version, parameter digest, and replay source</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/level-3-independent.yaml">
      <span class="site-pill hot">Add a gate</span>
      <strong>Level 3, independence and decision fields present</strong>
      <span>Claimed independent rejection authority, commitment boundary, and named accountable owner</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="labs-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="labs-title">Practice in the real labs</h2>
    <p>The cumulative cards above are small synthetic schema and replay fixtures. They isolate the contract, but they do not replace empirical lab work with tool-produced evidence.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/tree/main/labs">
      <span class="site-pill hot">Simulator-backed</span>
      <strong>Run the Architecture 2.0 labs</strong>
      <span>Generate evidence, inspect raw outputs, preserve failed runs and rejected alternatives, and submit a reviewable runnable receipt</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/book/chapters/08-running-the-loop/">
      <span class="site-pill">Synthesis</span>
      <strong>Study the Chapter 8 lighthouse example</strong>
      <span>Follow the constructed worked example that connects the complete loop</span>
    </a>
  </div>
  <p class="site-lede">The fixture progression and the real labs serve different teaching roles. Neither replaces Chapter 8's constructed lighthouse example.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="axes-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="axes-title">Keep three axes separate</h2>
    <p>A higher card level does not automatically mean higher-fidelity evidence or permission to make a larger commitment.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <div class="artifact-link">
      <span class="site-pill">Contract</span>
      <strong>Conformance</strong>
      <span>Which required record structures are present. Levels 0 to 3 and the schema validator measure this axis.</span>
    </div>
    <div class="artifact-link">
      <span class="site-pill">Measurement</span>
      <strong>Evidence fidelity</strong>
      <span>How closely the workload, tool, and observations represent the target system. A Level 3 card can still contain proxy evidence.</span>
    </div>
    <div class="artifact-link">
      <span class="site-pill">Decision</span>
      <strong>Commitment</strong>
      <span>What next action a human authorizes and how reversible it is. Stronger commitments require stronger evidence, not merely more card fields.</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <p class="site-lede">Disclosure and autonomy are separate review constraints too. Evidence may be confidential yet independently reviewable, and a loop documented by a Level 3 card may still grant the method very little autonomy.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="setup-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="setup-title">Set up the validator once</h2>
    <p>The isolated environment installs only the card validator. It does not install the book, PDF, plotting, or notebook toolchain.</p>
  </div>
  <pre class="start-command"><code>git clone https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2.git
cd arch2
python3 -m venv .venv-card
.venv-card/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip==26.1.2
.venv-card/bin/python -m pip install -r requirements-card.txt
.venv-card/bin/python cli/arch2.py validate card my-card.yaml</code></pre>
  <p class="start-step-note">The validator rejects unsupported schema versions, unknown fields, missing level requirements, and a dependent Level 3 rejection gate. See <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#building-the-site-locally">local project setup</a> for the complete development environment.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="artifacts-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="artifacts-title">Working artifacts</h2>
    <p>The schema defines conformance. The rubric and evidence review determine whether the card deserves trust.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/schemas/design-loop-card.v1.1.schema.json">
      <span class="site-pill">Contract</span>
      <strong>JSON Schema v1.1</strong>
      <span>Machine-readable field and conformance rules</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/design-loop-card/template.md" download>
      <span class="site-pill">Review</span>
      <strong>One-page Markdown form</strong>
      <span>A human-readable card for paper and project review</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/schemas/design-loop-card.md">
      <span class="site-pill">Compatibility</span>
      <strong>Version policy</strong>
      <span>Extension, compatibility, and change rules</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/tree/main/examples/design-loop-cards">
      <span class="site-pill">Replay</span>
      <strong>Evidence packet instructions</strong>
      <span>Render and verify the Level 2 and Level 3 example evidence</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section instructor-exercise" aria-labelledby="exercise-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="exercise-title">Instructor exercise</h2>
    <p>Pairs can complete a Level 0 paper-to-loop draft in one 30-minute block.</p>
  </div>
  <ol>
    <li><strong>Choose, 5 minutes.</strong> Select one paper with a concrete architecture result and name the next decision its result could support.</li>
    <li><strong>Bound, 10 minutes.</strong> Write the objective, constraints, non-goals, task, claim, and non-claim.</li>
    <li><strong>Separate, 10 minutes.</strong> List legal, invalid, and deferred design choices without reading the other pair's card.</li>
    <li><strong>Challenge, 5 minutes.</strong> Exchange cards and identify one hidden assumption or ambiguous boundary.</li>
  </ol>
  <p class="start-step-note">For a follow-on audit exercise, give students an existing evidence packet and ask them to build Level 1. Do not ask them to fabricate evidence or failed alternatives from a paper that does not report them.</p>
</section>
</div>
```
